title: Tests 2
description: The `assert_eq!` and `assert_ne!` macros compare two values and provide better error messages than `assert!`.
instructions: Use assert_eq! to test the function output.
hints: assert_eq! takes two arguments and checks they are equal.
explanation: `assert_eq!(left, right)` checks that two values are equal and panics with a message showing both values if they differ. `assert_ne!` checks that values are NOT equal. Both macros require the values to implement the `PartialEq` and `Debug` traits (most standard types do). The order of arguments doesn't matter, but convention is (expected, actual).
